cities.name: Dallas / Arlington
cities.advisory: Do not choose downtown lodging without a confirmed matchday transfer.
cities.stadiumReality: AT&T Stadium is in Arlington, not downtown Dallas. Travelers staying in Dallas should confirm matchday transport before leaving their hotel.
cities.heatCopy: June and July temperatures in Dallas regularly exceed 35–38°C. Plan hydration, sunscreen, shaded waiting time, and post-match exit delays.

transportOptions.warning: Confirm booking before matchday. This is the most predictable option.
transportOptions.action: Check official host city site for shuttle booking.

transportOptions.warning: Post-match surge pricing can be significant. Pickup zones may be far from exits. Expect 60–90+ min waits.
transportOptions.action: Have a backup plan. Do not rely on rideshare as your only option.

transportOptions.warning: Parking must be pre-booked. Road closures and post-match traffic can extend exit times significantly.
transportOptions.action: Pre-book parking only. No walk-up parking assumption.

transportOptions.warning: No direct rail connection between downtown Dallas and AT&T Stadium.
transportOptions.action: Do not assume rail will solve matchday movement.

hotelZones.pros: Best restaurant and nightlife access, easy airport connection
hotelZones.risks: No rail to stadium, rideshare surge likely, 20-mile transfer each way
hotelZones.action: Confirm shuttle or transfer before booking downtown hotel.

hotelZones.risks: Limited dining and city experience outside matchdays
hotelZones.action: Good option if stadium access is your priority.

hotelZones.risks: Far from both downtown and stadium, requires two transfers
hotelZones.action: Only if flight timing makes it necessary.

hotelZones.risks: Adds complexity to stadium transfer on matchday
hotelZones.action: Plan transfer carefully. Not walking distance to stadium.

dynamicFields.bagPolicy: Check official stadium policy before matchday.

cities.name: Boston / Foxborough
cities.advisory: Treat this as a regional trip, not a simple Boston city match.
cities.stadiumReality: Gillette Stadium is in Foxborough, approximately 30 miles south of Boston. It is a suburban venue requiring regional transport planning.
cities.heatCopy: Boston summer temperatures are generally mild. Heat is a lower risk here than other host cities.

transportOptions.warning: Special matchday service will run but capacity is limited. Book early.
transportOptions.action: Check MBTA matchday schedule. This is the recommended option.

transportOptions.warning: Confirm booking and pickup location before matchday.
transportOptions.action: Check official host city site for shuttle details.

transportOptions.warning: Parking is limited and must be pre-booked. Post-match exit is slow.
transportOptions.action: Pre-book only. Expect significant post-match delays.

hotelZones.pros: Best city experience, walkable, good dining
hotelZones.risks: Requires commuter rail or shuttle to stadium, 30 miles away
hotelZones.action: Confirm MBTA matchday service before booking.

hotelZones.risks: Very limited hotel options, book early
hotelZones.action: Book immediately if this is your preference. Limited supply.
Only if Boston hotels are fully booked.

dynamicFields.bagPolicy: Check official stadium policy before matchday.

cities.name: Miami
cities.advisory: Build your plan around heat, traffic, and the post-match exit.
cities.stadiumReality: Hard Rock Stadium is in Miami Gardens, not Miami Beach or downtown Miami. Visitors staying in popular tourist zones face a transfer.
cities.heatCopy: Miami in June and July combines heat with high humidity. Heat index can exceed 40°C. Plan accordingly for queues, outdoor waits, and post-match exits.

transportOptions.warning: Confirm booking before matchday.
transportOptions.action: Check official host city site for shuttle details.

transportOptions.warning: Post-match surge pricing likely. Heat makes outdoor waiting uncomfortable.
transportOptions.action: Plan a shaded waiting area. Have a backup option.

transportOptions.warning: Metrorail does not directly serve Hard Rock Stadium.
transportOptions.action: Do not assume Metrorail solves matchday movement.

transportOptions.warning: Post-match traffic on major routes is significant.
transportOptions.action: Pre-book parking. Expect long post-match exit.

hotelZones.risks: Furthest from stadium, bridge traffic adds time
hotelZones.action: Plan transfer carefully. Not a quick matchday hop.
Confirm shuttle or rideshare plan before matchday.
Good option if matchday logistics are your priority.

dynamicFields.bagPolicy: Check official stadium policy before matchday.

cities.name: New York / New Jersey
cities.infographicCaption: NYC core to New Jersey stadium
cities.advisory: Plan the stadium transfer as a regional movement, not a quick city hop.
cities.stadiumReality: MetLife Stadium is in East Rutherford, New Jersey. Travelers staying in Manhattan cross a state line to reach the venue.
cities.heatCopy: New York in June and July can be hot and humid. Heat is a moderate factor, particularly for post-match outdoor waits.

transportOptions.warning: Special matchday service will run. Expect very high volume.
transportOptions.action: Check NJ Transit matchday schedule. Arrive at Penn Station early.

transportOptions.warning: Cross-state rideshare adds cost. Post-match surge likely.
transportOptions.action: NJ Transit is the recommended option over rideshare.

transportOptions.warning: Driving into the NYC area on matchday is not recommended.
transportOptions.action: Use rail. Do not drive unless staying in New Jersey.

hotelZones.risks: NJ Transit transfer required, Penn Station crowds
hotelZones.action: Use NJ Transit. Allow extra time for Penn Station on matchday.
Good option if you prioritize matchday logistics.
NJ Transit connects to stadium. Reasonable option.

dynamicFields.bagPolicy: Check official stadium policy before matchday.

cities.name: Kansas City
cities.advisory: Do not assume standard city transit will solve matchday movement.
cities.stadiumReality: Arrowhead Stadium is southeast of downtown Kansas City. The city has limited public transit infrastructure and matchday movement is largely shuttle or car dependent.
cities.heatCopy: Kansas City in June and July averages 30–33°C. Heat is a moderate-to-high factor for outdoor queues.

transportOptions.warning: This is the primary recommended option. Confirm before matchday.
transportOptions.action: Check official host city site for shuttle details.

transportOptions.warning: Parking available but must be pre-booked.
transportOptions.action: Pre-book parking. Plan post-match traffic delays.

transportOptions.warning: Post-match surge likely. Limited pickup infrastructure near stadium.
transportOptions.action: Have a backup plan. Do not rely solely on rideshare.

transportOptions.warning: Kansas City public transit does not reliably serve the stadium on matchday.
transportOptions.action: Do not assume city bus will solve matchday movement.

hotelZones.risks: Shuttle or car required to stadium
hotelZones.action: Confirm shuttle plan before booking downtown.
Good if matchday logistics are priority.

dynamicFields.bagPolicy: Check official stadium policy before matchday.

cities.name: Atlanta
cities.infographicCaption: Stadium is downtown — MARTA crowds are the friction
cities.advisory: MARTA connects downtown to the stadium but post-match crush requires planning.
cities.stadiumReality: Mercedes-Benz Stadium is in downtown Atlanta, adjacent to the MARTA rail network. This is one of the better-connected venues but post-match MARTA crush is the primary friction.
cities.heatCopy: Atlanta in June and July averages 30–32°C with high humidity. Plan accordingly for outdoor queues.

transportOptions.warning: MARTA is the recommended option but post-match crush is significant.
transportOptions.action: Use MARTA. Plan to wait 20–30 min post-match before boarding.

transportOptions.warning: Post-match surge pricing. Pickup zones congested.
transportOptions.action: MARTA is better. Use rideshare only if staying far from rail.

transportOptions.warning: I-285 and downtown Atlanta roads are heavily congested post-match.
transportOptions.action: Avoid driving. Use MARTA.

hotelZones.pros: Walking distance or one MARTA stop to stadium
hotelZones.action: Best option for matchday logistics.
Reasonable option. Use MARTA.

hotelZones.risks: MARTA ride to stadium is long
hotelZones.action: Only if arrival or departure timing requires it.

dynamicFields.bagPolicy: Check official stadium policy before matchday.

cities.name: Houston
cities.advisory: Houston is a car-dependent city. Plan every transfer explicitly.
cities.stadiumReality: NRG Stadium is south of downtown Houston. The city is heavily car-dependent and public transit options to the stadium are limited.
cities.heatCopy: Houston in June and July averages 34–36°C with very high humidity. This is among the highest heat risk venues. Plan accordingly.

transportOptions.warning: Primary recommended option for non-drivers.
transportOptions.action: Confirm shuttle booking before matchday.

transportOptions.warning: Pre-booked parking available near NRG.
transportOptions.action: Pre-book parking. Plan post-match delays.

transportOptions.warning: METRORail does not directly serve NRG Stadium.
transportOptions.action: Do not assume rail solves matchday movement.

transportOptions.warning: Post-match surge likely. Heat makes outdoor waiting high risk.
transportOptions.action: Have indoor or shaded waiting option. Do not rely solely on rideshare.

hotelZones.action: Confirm shuttle or transfer plan before booking.
Good if matchday logistics are your priority.

dynamicFields.bagPolicy: Check official stadium policy before matchday.

cities.name: Los Angeles
cities.infographicCaption: LA sprawl — SoFi is in Inglewood
cities.advisory: Los Angeles traffic on matchday is a serious planning factor.
cities.stadiumReality: SoFi Stadium is in Inglewood, approximately 18 miles from downtown LA. LA traffic is unpredictable and matchday adds significant congestion.
cities.heatCopy: Los Angeles in June and July averages 25–28°C. Heat is a moderate factor, lower than Dallas or Miami.

transportOptions.warning: Confirm booking before matchday.
transportOptions.action: Check official host city site for shuttle details.

transportOptions.warning: Metro K Line serves Inglewood but matchday capacity is uncertain.
transportOptions.action: Check Metro matchday service. Allow extra time.

transportOptions.warning: I-405 on matchday is extremely slow. Post-match exit can take 90+ min.
transportOptions.action: Avoid driving on I-405. Use shuttle or Metro.

transportOptions.warning: Surge pricing likely. LA rideshare distances add significant cost.
transportOptions.action: Budget for high fares. Have a backup plan.

hotelZones.action: Use Metro K Line or shuttle. Avoid driving.
Shuttle is best option from this zone.
Good if matchday logistics are priority.
Use Metro or shuttle. Do not drive matchday.

dynamicFields.bagPolicy: Check official stadium policy before matchday.

cities.name: Seattle
cities.infographicCaption: Best transit access of all host cities
cities.advisory: Seattle is the most transit-friendly host city. Plan for post-match pedestrian volume.
cities.stadiumReality: Lumen Field is in downtown Seattle, walking distance from the city center and well-served by Link Light Rail and buses.
cities.heatCopy: Seattle in June and July averages 20–23°C. Heat is not a significant factor here.

transportOptions.warning: Post-match volume is high but service is frequent.
transportOptions.action: Use Link Light Rail. Most reliable option.

transportOptions.warning: Many hotels are within walking distance.
transportOptions.action: Check if your hotel is walkable. Easiest option.

transportOptions.warning: Post-match surge possible but less severe than other cities.
transportOptions.action: Reasonable backup if not using rail.

transportOptions.warning: Downtown Seattle parking is limited and expensive.
transportOptions.action: Use transit. Do not drive to downtown Seattle on matchday.

hotelZones.pros: Walking distance to stadium, best city access
hotelZones.action: Best option overall for Seattle matches.

hotelZones.pros: Good dining scene, close to downtown
hotelZones.risks: Short rail or rideshare to stadium
hotelZones.action: Reasonable option. Easy transit connection.
Allow extra time. Use Link Light Rail via downtown.

dynamicFields.bagPolicy: Check official stadium policy before matchday.

cities.name: Philadelphia
cities.infographicCaption: SEPTA works — post-match volume is the friction
cities.advisory: SEPTA connects downtown to the stadium but post-match volume requires patience.
cities.stadiumReality: Lincoln Financial Field is in South Philadelphia, approximately 4 miles from Center City. SEPTA connects the two but post-match crowds are heavy.
cities.heatCopy: Philadelphia in June and July averages 27–30°C. Heat is a moderate factor for outdoor post-match waits.

transportOptions.warning: Post-match crush is significant. Expect 20–40 min platform waits.
transportOptions.action: Use SEPTA. Plan to wait post-match rather than rushing to platform.

transportOptions.warning: Confirm booking before matchday.
transportOptions.action: Check official host city site for shuttle details.

transportOptions.warning: Post-match surge likely in South Philadelphia.
transportOptions.action: SEPTA is better. Use rideshare only as backup.

transportOptions.warning: South Philadelphia post-match traffic is heavily congested.
transportOptions.action: Avoid driving. Use SEPTA.

hotelZones.pros: Best city access, SEPTA connection to stadium
hotelZones.action: Best option. Use SEPTA Broad Street Line.

hotelZones.risks: Limited hotel options, neighborhood unfamiliar to tourists
hotelZones.action: Good if matchday logistics are priority.
Only if flight timing requires it.

dynamicFields.bagPolicy: Check official stadium policy before matchday.
